symptoms.symptom_name: Palpitations / racing, pounding heart with nausea, deadline-triggered

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Situationally triggered palpitations that ease with reassurance are typical of an autonomic panic surge.
Palpitations alone are non-specific; without ischaemic pain or exertional trigger they do not support a cardiac event.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symptoms tightly coupled to an imminent occupational deadline, easing with reassurance

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clear situational trigger with reassurance-responsiveness is characteristic of situational panic rather than a fixed organic cause.
Acute, trigger-bound, resolving anxiety is not the persistent pervasive worry of GAD.

symptoms.symptom_name: No ischaemic chest pain, no radiation to arm/jaw, no rest breathlessness (pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of ischaemic-type pain, radiation and exertional/rest breathlessness makes an acute coronary event unlikely, though in-person/ECG exclusion is still the gold standard.
Diaphoresis and agitation are part of the sympathetic arousal of an acute panic attack.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals: BP 145/90, HR 105 (sinus tachycardia), afebrile, SpO2 100%

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild anxiety-driven hypertension and sinus tachycardia that settle with reassurance — sealed clinician measurement; the AI infers arousal from reported palpitations, not a measured BP/HR.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal comprehensive physical/musculoskeletal exam, normal Snellen 6/6 and Ishihara colour vision

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute situational panic attack complicating a pre-employment occupational medical request
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms fitness for duty on objective testing — but these are clinician-performed occupational-assessment tasks that cannot be done over telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Supervised point-of-care 6-panel urine drug/alcohol screen — negative (including THC)

investigation_details.expected_result: Negative for all substances including THC
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without a supervised chain-of-custody screen the employer's fitness requirement is unmet; the AI cannot conduct or certify this remotely.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative supervised screen excludes intoxication as a cause of the tachycardia — a sealed in-person/lab result unavailable to telehealth in real time.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal pupillary response and coherent speech

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal pupils and coherent speech argue against a sympathomimetic intoxication — a clinician examination finding, not telehealth-obtainable.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Deadline-coupled palpitations, agitation and diaphoresis that ease with reassurance form a coherent benign situational-panic picture, distinct from a cardiac or toxicological emergency.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person occupational pre-employment medical (physical/MSK exam, visual acuity, colour vision) at a GP/occupational-health clinic
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Fitness-for-duty certification requires an in-person assessment appropriate to a heavy-machinery role — this is the substantive task the telehealth encounter must hand off, not complete.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Supervised AS/NZS 4308 urine drug/alcohol screen at an accredited collection service
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Workplace screening needs a supervised, chain-of-custody collection; it cannot be conducted or certified remotely.
Not indicated for a benign, reassurance-responsive situational tachycardia with no ischaemic features; reserve for genuine cardiac red flags (see safety-net).
